description: A grab-bag of developer quick-reference material: Big-O complexity, data structures, everyday Git commands, HTTP status codes, and regular-expression tokens. Five short decks, each a self-contained cheat sheet you drill until it's reflex.
primer:
## How this set works
Five cheat-sheet decks for things developers look up constantly. Every card's answer is in the tables below.

## Big-O complexity
From fastest to slowest: $O(1)$ constant · $O(\log n)$ logarithmic · $O(n)$ linear · $O(n\log n)$ linearithmic · $O(n^2)$ quadratic · $O(2^n)$ exponential.
- Binary search $O(\log n)$ (sorted data); linear search $O(n)$.
- Quicksort $O(n\log n)$ average, $O(n^2)$ worst; merge sort & heap sort $O(n\log n)$ always.
- Bubble/insertion sort $O(n^2)$. Comparison sorting is bounded below by $O(n\log n)$.
- Hash lookup $O(1)$ average / $O(n)$ worst; array index $O(1)$; linked-list search $O(n)$.
- Balanced-BST and heap ops $O(\log n)$; graph BFS/DFS $O(V+E)$.

## Data structures
- **Array**: contiguous; $O(1)$ index, $O(n)$ middle insert. Dynamic array appends $O(1)$ amortised.
- **Linked list**: $O(n)$ search, $O(1)$ insert/delete at a known node. Doubly-linked adds back-pointers.
- **Stack**: LIFO, $O(1)$ push/pop. **Queue**: FIFO, $O(1)$ enqueue/dequeue. **Deque**: both ends $O(1)$.
- **Hash map / set**: $O(1)$ average ops, $O(n)$ worst under collisions, order not preserved.
- **BST**: $O(\log n)$ if balanced, $O(n)$ if skewed; self-balancing (red-black/AVL) keeps $O(\log n)$ worst case.
- **Binary heap**: $O(\log n)$ push/pop, $O(1)$ peek root — backs a priority queue. **Trie**: $O(m)$ per key of length $m$.
- **Graph**: adjacency list ($O(V+E)$ space, sparse) vs adjacency matrix ($O(V^2)$, $O(1)$ edge check, dense).

## Git commands
- Stage: `git add -A`. Commit: `git commit -m "msg"`. Amend last: `git commit --amend`.
- Undo last commit keep staged: `git reset --soft HEAD~1`; discard: `git reset --hard HEAD~1`.
- Restore a file: `git restore <file>`; unstage: `git restore --staged <file>`.
- Reverse a pushed commit safely: `git revert <commit>`. Recover lost commits: `git reflog`.
- Branch+switch: `git switch -c <name>`; switch: `git switch <name>`; delete merged: `git branch -d <name>`.
- `git merge <branch>` (merge commit) vs `git rebase <branch>` (linear, rewrites hashes). One commit: `git cherry-pick <commit>`.
- Inspect: `git status`, `git log --oneline`, `git diff`. Shelve work: `git stash` / `git stash pop`.
- Remotes: `git fetch` (no merge), `git pull` (fetch+merge), `git push`, `git clone <url>`.

## HTTP status codes
- **2xx success**: 200 OK · 201 Created · 202 Accepted (async) · 204 No Content.
- **3xx redirection**: 301 Moved Permanently · 302 Found (temporary) · 304 Not Modified (cache) · 307 Temporary Redirect (keep method) · 308 Permanent Redirect (keep method).
- **4xx client error**: 400 Bad Request · 401 Unauthorized (authenticate) · 403 Forbidden (not allowed) · 404 Not Found · 405 Method Not Allowed · 409 Conflict · 418 I'm a teapot (joke) · 422 Unprocessable Entity (validation) · 429 Too Many Requests (rate limit).
- **5xx server error**: 500 Internal Server Error · 502 Bad Gateway · 503 Service Unavailable · 504 Gateway Timeout.

## Regex tokens
- **Classes**: `.` any char (not newline) · `\d` digit · `\w` word char (letter/digit/_) · `\s` whitespace · `[abc]` set · `[^abc]` negated set · `[a-z]` range. Uppercase `\D \W \S` negate.
- **Anchors**: `^` start · `$` end · `\b` word boundary · `\B` non-boundary (all zero-width).
- **Quantifiers**: `*` zero+ · `+` one+ · `?` zero/one (optional) · `{n,m}` n to m times. A trailing `?` (e.g. `*?`) makes it lazy/minimal.
- **Groups & lookarounds**: `(abc)` capturing · `(?:abc)` non-capturing · `a|b` alternation · `(?=...)` positive lookahead · `(?!...)` negative lookahead.

Know these tables and you'll pass every deck.
